api/v3/handlers/addons.purpose: HTTP handlers for the v3 addon CRUD and lifecycle (publish/archive) endpoints, translating between apiv3.Addon wire types and addon.Service domain calls via the httptransport pipeline. Each operation lives in its own dedicated file.

patterns.name: One operation per file
patterns.description: Each CRUD or lifecycle operation lives in its own file (create.go, delete.go, get.go, list.go, update.go, archive.go, publish.go). handler.go only declares the Handler interface and constructor.
patterns.example: func (h *handler) ArchiveAddon() ArchiveAddonHandler { return httptransport.NewHandlerWithArgs(...) }

patterns.name: Type alias triad per operation
patterns.description: Every operation file declares three type aliases: <Op>Request = domain.Input, <Op>Response = apiv3.Type, <Op>Handler = httptransport.Handler[Req,Resp] (or HandlerWithArgs for path-param operations).
patterns.example: type ( ArchiveAddonRequest = addon.ArchiveAddonInput; ArchiveAddonResponse = apiv3.Addon; ArchiveAddonHandler httptransport.HandlerWithArgs[...] )

patterns.name: resolveNamespace in every decoder
patterns.description: All decoders call h.resolveNamespace(ctx) as the first step and propagate the error before building the domain input struct.
patterns.example: ns, err := h.resolveNamespace(ctx); if err != nil { return ArchiveAddonRequest{}, err }

patterns.name: apierrors.GenericErrorEncoder as base error handler
patterns.description: Every httptransport.AppendOptions call includes httptransport.WithErrorEncoder(apierrors.GenericErrorEncoder()) as the error handler.
patterns.example: httptransport.WithErrorEncoder(apierrors.GenericErrorEncoder())

patterns.name: convert.go owns all domain<->API mapping
patterns.description: All ToAPI* and FromAPI* conversion functions are in convert.go; individual operation files call these helpers, never inline-converting types.
patterns.example: return ToAPIAddon(*a) // in operation handler body

patterns.name: Nil-check after service call
patterns.description: Handlers that receive a pointer result (*addon.Addon) guard against nil before calling ToAPIAddon, returning a descriptive error instead.
patterns.example: if a == nil { return ArchiveAddonResponse{}, fmt.Errorf("failed to archive add-on") }

patterns.name: IgnoreNonCriticalIssues on mutating inputs
patterns.description: Create and update decoders set req.IgnoreNonCriticalIssues = true after FromAPI conversion to suppress non-fatal validation issues.
patterns.example: req.IgnoreNonCriticalIssues = true

key_file_guides.role: Declares the Handler interface listing all endpoint methods and the handler struct with resolveNamespace, service addon.Service, and options []httptransport.HandlerOption. New() constructor.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a new endpoint: add it to the Handler interface here first, then create a dedicated file.

key_file_guides.role: All bidirectional conversion between apiv3 and productcatalog/addon domain types including rate cards, prices (flat/unit/tiered), tax config, discounts, and labels.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Rate card type switch is exhaustive — adding a new RateCardType requires a new case in both ToAPIBillingRateCard and FromAPIBillingRateCard.

key_file_guides.role: ListAddons with page-pagination using pagination.NewPage defaults (1, 20); validates page before building ListAddonsInput.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Default page size is 20; page validation errors must use apierrors.NewBadRequestError with field='page'.

key_file_guides.role: ArchiveAddon sets EffectiveTo: clock.Now() in the decoder — the HTTP layer owns wall-clock time, not the domain service.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Lifecycle operations (publish, archive) must set clock.Now() in the decoder, not inside the service call.

anti_patterns: Inline type conversion in operation files instead of calling ToAPIAddon/FromAPICreate*
Returning domain errors directly without httptransport error encoder chain
Skipping h.resolveNamespace(ctx) call in a new operation decoder
Using httptransport.NewHandler instead of NewHandlerWithArgs for path-param operations
Adding business logic (validation, defaults) outside of the domain service or convert.go

decisions.decision: Lifecycle operations (publish, archive) set clock.Now() in the decoder, not the service.
decisions.rationale: HTTP layer owns wall-clock time for request-scoped operations; domain service receives explicit timestamps making it easier to test.

decisions.decision: convert.go handles all price discriminated union encoding/decoding manually (not goverter).
decisions.rationale: BillingPrice is a oneOf union requiring Discriminator() + From* calls that goverter cannot generate; manual switch-case is the only safe option.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new addon lifecycle endpoint (e.g., RestoreAddon)

api/v3/handlers/apps.purpose: HTTP handlers for listing and getting installed billing apps (Stripe, Sandbox, CustomInvoicing) in the v3 API; performs polymorphic type-assertion on app.App to map each concrete type to the BillingApp union discriminated type.

patterns.name: Polymorphic app mapping via type-assertion switch
patterns.description: ToAPIBillingApp in convert.go switches on item.GetType(), type-asserts to the concrete app (appstripe.App, appsandbox.App, appcustominvoicing.App), and calls From* on api.BillingApp union.
patterns.example: billingApp := api.BillingApp{}; if err := billingApp.FromBillingAppStripe(billingAppStripe); err != nil { ... }

patterns.name: Goverter for simple struct conversions, manual code for discriminated unions
patterns.description: convert.go carries goverter:variables annotations; convert.gen.go is generated. Simple field-to-field mappings are goverter-generated; the polymorphic ToAPIBillingApp is hand-written.
patterns.example: //go:generate go run github.com/jmattheis/goverter/cmd/goverter gen ./

patterns.name: handler struct holds app.Service (not a domain-specific service)
patterns.description: Unlike other handlers that hold a domain-specific service, the apps handler holds app.Service (the top-level app registry service).
patterns.example: type handler struct { resolveNamespace ...; appService app.Service; options ... }

patterns.name: var _ Handler = (*handler)(nil) compile-time check
patterns.description: handler.go includes an explicit interface satisfaction check to catch missing method implementations at compile time.
patterns.example: var _ Handler = (*handler)(nil)

key_file_guides.role: Hand-written ToAPIBillingApp (polymorphic switch) + goverter annotations for list/catalog conversions. Adding a new app type requires a new case in the switch and a new toAPIBillingApp<Type> helper.
key_file_guides.watch_for: goverter:enum:map annotations must be kept in sync with app.AppType constants; missing entries cause @error at runtime.

key_file_guides.role: Generated by goverter — DO NOT EDIT. Regenerate with 'go generate ./...' after modifying convert.go annotations.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Editing this file manually will be overwritten on next generate.

key_file_guides.role: Handler interface (ListApps, GetApp) + New() constructor. Interface satisfaction checked with var _ Handler = (*handler)(nil).
key_file_guides.watch_for: New app endpoints must be added to the Handler interface.

anti_patterns: Adding a new app type without a case in the ToAPIBillingApp switch
Editing convert.gen.go directly instead of regenerating
Accessing app-specific fields without type-asserting to the concrete app type first
Bypassing the ToAPIBillingApp polymorphic helper to do inline conversion in get_app.go or list_app.go

decisions.decision: BillingApp is a oneOf union; mapping requires app.App interface → concrete type assertion → From* call on the union.
decisions.rationale: The v3 OpenAPI union type requires calling the generated From* discriminator method; no generic field copy works across different concrete types.

decisions.decision: Goverter is used only for struct-level mappings (PagePaginatedResponse, BillingAppCatalogItem); the discriminated union is hand-coded.
decisions.rationale: Goverter cannot handle interface type-switches or oneOf union encoding.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new billing app type mapping

api/v3/handlers/billingprofiles.purpose: HTTP handlers for billing profile CRUD in the v3 API; bridges billing.Service (ProfileService) to HTTP with a domain-specific error encoder and goverter-generated conversions for workflow configs, tax config, and supplier parties.

patterns.name: Domain-specific error encoder chained with GenericErrorEncoder
patterns.description: Every operation in this package appends both apierrors.GenericErrorEncoder() AND the local errorEncoder() which maps billing.NotFoundError→404, billing.ValidationError→400, billing.UpdateAfterDeleteError→409.
patterns.example: httptransport.WithErrorEncoder(apierrors.GenericErrorEncoder()), httptransport.WithErrorEncoder(errorEncoder())

patterns.name: Goverter context params for namespace injection
patterns.description: Goverter-generated FromAPICreateBillingProfileRequest accepts a string context param (namespace) which gets mapped to the Namespace field via goverter:context and NamespaceFromContext helper.

patterns.name: ProfileExpand: Apps set on read operations
patterns.description: GetBillingProfile and ListBillingProfiles always set billing.ProfileExpand{Apps: true} so the app references are populated in the response.
patterns.example: Expand: billing.ProfileExpand{Apps: true}

patterns.name: error_encoder.go isolates billing error mapping
patterns.description: A dedicated error_encoder.go file returns an encoder.ErrorEncoder closure that handles billing-specific error types, keeping operation files free of error type assertions.
patterns.example: commonhttp.HandleErrorIfTypeMatches[billing.NotFoundError](ctx, http.StatusNotFound, err, w, billing.EncodeValidationIssues)

key_file_guides.role: Goverter annotations + hand-written helpers (ToAPIBillingWorkflow, FromAPIBillingWorkflow, ToAPIBillingParty, FromAPIBillingParty, ToAPIBillingAppReference). FromAPIBillingWorkflow parses ISO duration strings and handles alignment discriminated union.
key_file_guides.watch_for: FromAPIBillingWorkflow uses period.Parse for duration strings and falls back to billing.DefaultWorkflowConfig; both collection alignment variants (subscription, anchored) must be handled.

key_file_guides.role: Maps billing.NotFoundError, billing.ValidationError, billing.UpdateAfterDeleteError, billing.ValidationIssue, billing.AppError to HTTP status codes using commonhttp.HandleErrorIfTypeMatches.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Order matters: more specific types should appear before more general ones in the chain.

key_file_guides.role: Generated by goverter — DO NOT EDIT. Contains FromAPICreateBillingProfileRequest, FromAPIUpsertBillingProfileRequest, ToAPIBillingProfile, ToAPIBillingProfiles, ToAPIBillingTaxConfig, FromAPIBillingTaxConfig.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Regenerate after any change to convert.go annotations.

key_file_guides.role: GetBillingProfile with ProfileExpand{Apps: true}; returns apierrors.NewNotFoundError if profile is nil after service call.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Nil profile check after service call must return apierrors.NewNotFoundError, not a generic error.

anti_patterns: Omitting errorEncoder() from any billing profile operation — GenericErrorEncoder alone won't map billing.NotFoundError to 404
Editing convert.gen.go manually
Forgetting ProfileExpand{Apps: true} on read paths that need app reference data
Adding new workflow fields to convert.go without updating both ToAPIBillingWorkflow and FromAPIBillingWorkflow

decisions.decision: A local errorEncoder() is provided alongside GenericErrorEncoder because billing errors (NotFoundError, ValidationError, UpdateAfterDeleteError) are domain-specific types not handled by the generic encoder.
decisions.rationale: The generic encoder handles models.Generic* errors; billing defines its own error types for richer validation issue propagation.

decisions.decision: Goverter context params are used to thread namespace and namespacedID into generated converters instead of manual construction.
decisions.rationale: Avoids boilerplate in every FromAPI function while keeping the generated code correct and auditable.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new billing profile operation with proper error encoding

api/v3/handlers/currencies.purpose: HTTP handlers for listing custom currencies and cost bases in the v3 API; uses namespacedriver.NamespaceDecoder (not a resolveNamespace closure) for namespace resolution and delegates to currencies.CurrencyService.

patterns.name: NamespaceDecoder instead of resolveNamespace closure
patterns.description: Unlike most handler packages that accept a resolveNamespace func(ctx) (string, error), this handler holds a namespacedriver.NamespaceDecoder and calls h.namespaceDecoder.GetNamespace(ctx) returning (string, bool).
patterns.example: ns, ok := h.namespaceDecoder.GetNamespace(ctx); if !ok { return ..., apierrors.NewInternalError(ctx, fmt.Errorf("failed to resolve namespace")) }

patterns.name: Page defaults differ by operation
patterns.description: ListCurrencies uses default page size 100 (not 20 like other handlers), reflecting expected currency catalog size.
patterns.example: page := pagination.NewPage(1, 100)

patterns.name: Handler interface groups both resource sub-types
patterns.description: The Handler interface includes both currency (ListCurrencies, CreateCurrency) and cost basis (CreateCostBasis, ListCostBases) endpoints in a single handler struct backed by currencies.CurrencyService.
patterns.example: type Handler interface { ListCurrencies() ...; CreateCurrency() ...; CreateCostBasis() ...; ListCostBases() ... }

patterns.name: BillingCurrency discriminated union via generic helper
patterns.description: convert.go uses NewBillingCurrencyFrom[T] generic to construct the BillingCurrency union for both custom and fiat types, calling From* discriminator methods.
patterns.example: func NewBillingCurrencyFrom[T v3.BillingCurrencyCustom | v3.BillingCurrencyFiat](v T) (v3.BillingCurrency, error)

key_file_guides.role: Handler interface + handler struct holding namespaceDecoder, currencyService currencies.CurrencyService, and options. New() constructor.
key_file_guides.watch_for: This handler uses namespacedriver.NamespaceDecoder not a func; new operations must call GetNamespace (returns bool, not error) not resolveNamespace.

key_file_guides.role: ListCurrencies with optional filter[type] query param mapped through FromAPIBillingCurrencyType and page-pagination with default size 100.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Filter is optional; nil check required before dereferencing params.Filter.

key_file_guides.role: ToAPIBillingCurrency distinguishes custom (has ID) from fiat (no ID) to route to the correct union branch; ToAPIBillingCostBasis converts the rate Decimal to string.
key_file_guides.watch_for: The custom vs fiat branch is determined by c.ID != '' — any currency with an empty ID is treated as fiat.

anti_patterns: Using resolveNamespace pattern instead of namespaceDecoder.GetNamespace
Defaulting page size to 20 (use 100 for currency catalog lists)
Mixing currency and cost-basis conversion logic into operation files rather than a convert.go
Forgetting the ok bool from GetNamespace and assuming it always returns a valid namespace

decisions.decision: namespacedriver.NamespaceDecoder is injected instead of a resolveNamespace closure because currencies was wired before the closure pattern was standardized.
decisions.rationale: Both patterns resolve namespace from context; the decoder pattern is older and specific to certain handler packages that predate the closure convention.

api/v3/handlers/events.purpose: HTTP handler for listing and ingesting CloudEvents in the v3 API; provides rich filter support (type, time, ingested_at, stored_at, customer_id) and sort with unit-tested filter parsing helpers.

patterns.name: Filter parsing helpers are package-private and unit-tested
patterns.description: fromAPICustomerIDFilter, fromAPIEventSort, and fromAPIEventFilter are unexported helpers in list.go with dedicated unit tests in list_test.go covering all supported and rejected filter variants.
patterns.example: func fromAPICustomerIDFilter(ctx context.Context, f *api.ULIDFieldFilter) (*filter.FilterString, error)

patterns.name: apierrors.NewBadRequestError with exact field path for filter errors
patterns.description: Every unsupported filter operator returns apierrors.NewBadRequestError with the exact query-param field path as the InvalidParameter.Field value.
patterns.example: apierrors.NewBadRequestError(ctx, err, apierrors.InvalidParameters{{Field: "filter[customer_id]", Reason: err.Error(), Source: apierrors.InvalidParamSourceQuery}})

patterns.name: Sort defaults to desc when no suffix provided
patterns.description: fromAPIEventSort parses the SortQuery string; a field name without an asc/desc suffix is treated as descending by default so sort=time means most-recent first.
patterns.example: sort := api.SortQuery("time") // → sortx.OrderDesc

patterns.name: Customer ID filter supports only Eq and Oeq operators
patterns.description: fromAPICustomerIDFilter explicitly rejects Neq, Contains, Ocontains, Exists operators with a descriptive error; only eq and oeq are forwarded as an IN set to the backend.
patterns.example: if f.Neq != nil { err := errors.New("only eq and oeq operators are supported"); return nil, apierrors.NewBadRequestError(...) }

key_file_guides.role: Main list events handler plus all filter/sort parsing helpers. fromAPICustomerIDFilter only supports Eq and Oeq; Neq/Contains/Ocontains/Exists are rejected.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a new filter field requires adding both a parsing helper and a test case in list_test.go.

key_file_guides.role: Unit tests for filter and sort parsing helpers using t.Context(). assertBadRequestField verifies the exact InvalidParameter.Field in error responses.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Tests use t.Context() not context.Background() — keep consistent.

key_file_guides.role: toAPIMeteringIngestedEvent converts meterevent.Event to API wire form; parses JSON data string to map[string]any; sets CloudEvents specversion=1.0.
key_file_guides.watch_for: e.Data is a raw JSON string — must unmarshal to map[string]any before assigning to nullable event.Data field.

key_file_guides.role: IngestEvents handler for CloudEvent ingestion path.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Ingest path is separate from list path; do not mix streaming connector calls with ingest collector calls.

anti_patterns: Supporting filter operators beyond Eq/Oeq for customer_id without adding explicit rejection tests for unsupported operators
Parsing filter defaults in the handler operation func instead of dedicated unexported helper functions
Returning raw errors from filter parsing instead of apierrors.NewBadRequestError with exact field path
Using context.Background() in list_test.go tests instead of t.Context()

decisions.decision: Filter parsing helpers are unexported and unit-tested in list_test.go rather than integration-tested.
decisions.rationale: Parser logic is deterministic and does not require a running service; unit tests give fast feedback on edge cases like malformed sort strings.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new filter field to the events list handler
